title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description:
## Longest Consecutive Sequence

Given an unsorted array of integers `nums`, return the length of the longest sequence of consecutive integers (e.g., `[1,2,3,4]`).

The algorithm must run in O(n) time.

**Constraints:**
- `0 <= nums.length <= 10^5`
- `-10^9 <= nums[i] <= 10^9`

solutions.label: Sorting — O(n log n)
solutions.explanation: Remove duplicates and sort. Then scan linearly: if each number is one more than the previous, extend the current streak; otherwise reset. Simple but not O(n).

solutions.label: Hash Set — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Put all numbers in a hash set for O(1) lookup. The key insight: only start counting a sequence from its actual beginning — a number `n` is a sequence start if `n-1` is not in the set. From each start, count forward as long as consecutive numbers exist. Even though there's a nested loop, each number is visited at most twice total across all sequences, giving O(n) overall.

solutions.label: Hash Set — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Only start counting from the beginning of a sequence (n-1 not in set). Each element is visited at most twice.

hints: Put all numbers in a hash set. For each number, check if it's the start of a sequence (num-1 is not in the set).
Only count sequences from their start — this ensures each element is visited at most once, giving O(n) time.
